heading: 1. Executive Summary
content: This guide provides a complete procedural framework for implementing Microsoft 365 Copilot analytics and monitoring. It covers five distinct reporting tiers, each serving different stakeholders with varying prerequisites and capabilities.

heading: Tier 1
content: M365 Admin Center Reports IT Admins License & usage tracking No setup needed →

heading: Tier 2
content: Copilot Dashboard Leaders & Managers ROI, Adoption, Sentiment 50+ licenses →

heading: Tier 3
content: Copilot Analytics Reports Leaders & Analysts Out-of-the-box reports Dashboard access →

heading: Tier 4
content: Agent Dashboard Leaders & Analysts Agent adoption & usage 50+ licenses →

heading: Tier 5
content: Advanced Analytics (Power BI) Analysts Custom queries & reports Viva Insights license Progressive implementation: Each tier builds on the previous. All tiers share organizational data and privacy settings.

heading: Readiness Page
content: Shows license assignment status and readiness for Copilot adoption. Available even with less than 50 licenses.

heading: Adoption Page
content: Displays group-level usage trends, active user counts, usage intensity, and consistent usage metrics. Supports internal and external benchmark comparisons. Requires 50+ assigned licenses for group filtering.

heading: Impact Page
content: Shows four top-level metrics: Copilot Actions Taken , Copilot Assisted Hours , Copilot Assisted Value , and Satisfaction Rate . This is the primary ROI and impact measurement page. A six-month trendline shows weekly snapshots of each metric (selectable via the Metric dropdown). Each metric is cal...

heading: Sentiment Section (within Impact Page)
content: Located within the Impact page, the Sentiment section displays your organisation's survey results alongside Microsoft's benchmark data from early Copilot users. It shows: Sentiment results by question — Your org's response to each of the four recommended Copilot questions, compared to Microsoft's...

heading: 4. Copilot Credits — Understanding Consumption
content: Copilot Credits are the consumption currency for Copilot Studio agent interactions and certain M365 Copilot Chat scenarios. Understanding what costs credits — and what doesn't — is essential for budget management.

heading: What Costs Credits
content: Agent responses using Copilot Studio capabilities beyond zero-rated usage Tenant data grounding (SharePoint, Graph) in M365 Copilot Chat (web-based) Custom agent actions, plugins, and connector invocations Generative answers using non-M365 knowledge sources

heading: Zero-Rated (No Credits Required)

heading: Credit Mechanics
content: Credits reset monthly — there is no rollover of unused credits to the next month. Overage behaviour: when credits are exhausted, model runs are blocked until more credits are purchased or allocated. Credits are pooled at the tenant level for prepaid plans, or billed per-environment for pay-as-you...
